title: OER partner/platform outreach + pilot unit shortlist
context: No adoption partner is currently secured. All authoring tasks carry verifiedNeed:false until a named OER platform, school, or teacher agrees to review and use a bank. The Elyos bar is 'delivered, not merged' — a bank that is produced but never adopted is not a good deed delivered. This task opens the outreach threads that feed partner-013 (first confirmed adoption partner) and selects the OER unit for the pilot bank (pilot-009) based on a realistic adoption path, a clearly permissive license, and a non-regulated subject area.
objective: Open outreach to candidate OER platforms and teachers, identify a shortlist of OER units with realistic adoption paths and permissive licenses, and select the pilot unit for quiz-bank-open-pilot-009.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one outreach thread opened with a named candidate OER platform or teacher (e.g. OpenStax, LibreTexts, CK-12, Saylor, Kolibri/Learning Equality, Oppia, or an individual teacher network).

A shortlist of candidate OER units documented, each with: source URL, publisher, license, subject area, learning objectives, and assessment of the adoption path (informal channel vs. self-serve fallback).
Pilot unit selected from the shortlist: clearly permissive license (CC-BY, CC0, or public domain), non-regulated subject (e.g. middle-school math or intro science), and a realistic adoption path.
Document committed to the project repo; CC-BY-4.0 licensed; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: OpenStax, LibreTexts, CK-12, Saylor Academy, Kolibri/Learning Equality, Oppia (candidate OER publishers/platforms)
output: A committed outreach log and pilot-unit shortlist with the selected pilot unit, adoption path, and license record.
